_doc: Shared gate for run_eval.py (and the report). Per metric: better = which direction is an improvement; a change is a REGRESSION when it moves the worse way by more than max(abs_floor, baseline*rel_tol). trigger = absolute value above which the metric counts as an active ISSUE on a clip (independent of any baseline).
_pop_note: pop_px_p50/p95 (median/p95 |dx|) are REPORTED (see sbsbench FMT) but deliberately NOT in 'metrics', so they don't gate: subject anchoring legitimately lowers median |dx| by recentering the field on the subject without losing depth. pop_spread_px (near-to-far RANGE) is the fair volume gate and also catches a genuinely flat pipeline.

score._doc: Overall 0-100 artifact quality. score equals q_clean = 100 - sum of artifact penalties, each = weight * min(value/scale, 1). q_depth separately reports realized stereo volume (or absence of false stereo for expected-flat clips); it does not add quality points because depth loss must not cancel an artifact improvement or vice versa. Feature decisions use per-clip metric tolerances, not score alone.

depth._doc: Diagnostic only: normal clips report stereo volume toward target=2.5%; expected-flat clips invert it and report absence of false stereo below 0.1%. pop_spread remains independently gated.
